revision.text:
'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん、'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''とも）は、[[大分県]][[別府市]]上人西町にある[[古墳]]。1957年11月28日に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]後期（6世紀後半）の円墳2基からなる装飾古墳群。1号墳は別府市立上人小学校の敷地内にあり、2号墳もそのすぐそばにある。[[安山岩]]の巨石を組み上げた大きな石室を持つことが特徴である。
1号墳は、墳丘は流失しており、その大きさを推定することは困難である。石室は、複室式の横穴式石室で、内部は全面が赤色に塗られ、前室には白色の鋸歯文が描かれている。また、近年、黄色や黒の三角文、蕨手文も確認されている。
2号墳は、1号墳の南西約80mにあり、直径約30m、高さ約6mの円墳である。石室は、単室の横穴式石室で、奥行き8m、玄室の天井高約4m。壁は石材をアーチ状に積層した構造を有する。玄室内部は、全面が赤色に塗装され、黒色で多数の円文、蕨文等が描かれている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://bep-shounin-e.oita-ed.jp/gakkousyoukai/setumei/oninoiwaya.htm 鬼の岩屋古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おにのいわやこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:大分県の史跡]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]] [[Category:別府市]]

'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん、'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''とも）は、[[大分県]][[別府市]]上人西町にある[[古墳]]。1957年11月28日に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。

1号墳は、墳丘は流失しており、その大きさを推定することは困難である。石室は、複室式の横穴式石室で、内部は全面が赤色に塗られ、前室には白色の鋸歯文が描かれている。また、近年、黄色や黒の三角文、蕨手文も確認されている。
2号墳は、1号墳の南西約80mにあり、直径約30m、高さ約6mの円墳である。石室は、単室の横穴式石室で、奥行き8m、玄室の天井高約4m。壁は石材をアーチ状に積層した構造を有する。玄室内部は、全面が赤色に塗装され、黒色で多数の円文、蕨文等が描かれている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://bep-shounin-e.oita-ed.jp/gakkousyoukai/setumei/oninoiwaya.htm 鬼の岩屋古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おにのいわやこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:大分県の史跡]] [[Category:別府市]]

1号墳は、墳丘は流失しており、その大きさを推定することは困難である。石室は、複室式の横穴式石室で、内部は全面が赤色に塗られ、前室には白色の鋸歯文が描かれている。また、近年、黄色や黒の三角文、蕨手文も確認されている。
2号墳は、1号墳の南西約80メートルにあり、直径約30メートル、高さ約6メートルの円墳である。石室は、単室の横穴式石室で、奥行き8メートル、玄室の天井高約4メートル。壁は石材をアーチ状に積層した構造を有する。玄室内部は、全面が赤色に塗装され、黒色で多数の円文、蕨文等が描かれている。

'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん、'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''とも）は、[[大分県]][[別府市]]上人西町にある[[古墳]]。1957年（昭和32年）11月28日に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]後期（6世紀後半）の[[円墳]]2基からなる[[装飾古墳]]群。1号墳は別府市立上人小学校の敷地内にあり、2号墳もそのすぐそばにある。[[安山岩]]の巨石を組み上げた大きな石室を持つことが特徴である。
1号墳は、墳丘は流失しており、その大きさを推定すると、径約23メートル、高さ5メートルほどの円墳。石室は、全長9.2メートル、玄室は奥行き、幅とも2.3メートル、高さ2.9メートルの複室式の[[横穴式石室]]で、内部は全面が赤色に塗られ、前室には白色の鋸歯文が描かれている。また、近年、黄色や黒の三角文、蕨手文も確認されている。
2号墳は、1号墳の南西約80メートルにあり、直径約30メートル、高さ約6メートルの円墳である。石室は、単室の横穴式石室で、奥行き8メートル、玄室の天井高約4メートル。壁は石材をアーチ状に積層した構造を有する。玄室内部は、全面が赤色に塗装され、黒色で多数の円文、蕨文等が描かれている。

revision.comment: 国の史跡に係る事柄について加筆。
revision.text:
'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん、'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''とも）は、[[大分県]][[別府市]]上人西町にある[[古墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群」のうち）。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]後期（6世紀後半）の[[円墳]]2基からなる[[装飾古墳]]群。1号墳は別府市立上人小学校の敷地内にあり、2号墳もそのすぐそばにある。[[安山岩]]の巨石を組み上げた大きな石室を持つことが特徴である。
1号墳は、墳丘は流失しており、その大きさを推定すると、径約23メートル、高さ5メートルほどの円墳。石室は、全長9.2メートル、玄室は奥行き、幅とも2.3メートル、高さ2.9メートルの複室式の[[横穴式石室]]で、内部は全面が赤色に塗られ、前室には白色の鋸歯文が描かれている。また、近年、黄色や黒の三角文、蕨手文も確認されている。
2号墳は、1号墳の南西約80メートルにあり、直径約30メートル、高さ約6メートルの円墳である。石室は、単室の横穴式石室で、奥行き8メートル、玄室の天井高約4メートル。壁は石材をアーチ状に積層した構造を有する。玄室内部は、全面が赤色に塗装され、黒色で多数の円文、蕨文等が描かれている。
== 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群 *: 1957年（昭和32年）11月28日に「鬼ノ岩屋古墳」として国の史跡に指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2880|鬼ノ岩屋古墳}}</ref>。 *: 2017年（平成29年）2月9日に史跡範囲を追加指定し、指定名称を「鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群」に変更<ref>平成29年2月9日文部科学省告示第12号。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版 1991年 ISBN 4-8104-0926-0
== 外部リンク == * [http://bep-shounin-e.oita-ed.jp/gakkousyoukai/setumei/oninoiwaya.htm 鬼の岩屋古墳] * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2880|鬼ノ岩屋古墳}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{coord|33|18|56.4|N|131|29|32.3|E|region:JP-44|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おにのいわやこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:大分県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:別府市の歴史]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

revision.text:
{{panoViewer|OniNoIwaya Kofun-2, Funkyu 360-degree.jpg|2号墳の全天球画像}} '''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん、'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''とも）は、[[大分県]][[別府市]]上人西町にある[[古墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群]]」のうち）。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]後期（6世紀後半）の[[円墳]]2基からなる[[装飾古墳]]群。1号墳は[[別府市立上人小学校]]の敷地内にあり、2号墳もそのすぐそばにある。[[安山岩]]の巨石を組み上げた大きな石室を持つことが特徴である。
1号墳は、[[墳丘]]は流失しており、その大きさを推定すると、径約23メートル、高さ5メートルほどの円墳。石室は、全長9.2メートル、玄室は奥行き、幅とも2.3メートル、高さ2.9メートルの複室式の[[横穴式石室]]で、内部は全面が赤色に塗られ、前室には白色の[[鋸歯文]]が描かれている。また、近年、黄色や黒の三角文、蕨手文も確認されている。
2号墳は、1号墳の南西約80メートルにあり、直径約30メートル、高さ約6メートルの円墳である。石室は、単室の横穴式石室で、奥行き8メートル、玄室の天井高約4メートル。壁は石材をアーチ状に積層した構造を有する。玄室内部は、全面が赤色に塗装され、黒色で多数の[[円文]]、[[蕨文]]等が描かれている。
== 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群 *: 1957年（昭和32年）11月28日に「鬼ノ岩屋古墳」として国の史跡に指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2880|鬼ノ岩屋古墳}}</ref>。 *: 2017年（平成29年）2月9日に史跡範囲を追加指定し、指定名称を「鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群」に変更<ref>平成29年2月9日文部科学省告示第12号。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版 1991年 ISBN 4-8104-0926-0
== 外部リンク == * [http://bep-shounin-e.oita-ed.jp/gakkousyoukai/setumei/oninoiwaya.htm 鬼の岩屋古墳] * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2880|鬼ノ岩屋古墳}}

{{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Beppu, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| burial mound]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/gakusyuu/bunkazai/bunkazai_kohun.html Beppu City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Supervised by the Historical Sites Research Committee of the Cultural Property Protection Division, Cultural Affairs Agency, *''Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan, Volume 3: Prehistoric 3''*, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, ISBN 4-8104-0926-0.

The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

{{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Beppu, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Supervised by the Historical Sites Research Committee of the Cultural Property Protection Division, Cultural Affairs Agency, *''Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan, Volume 3: Prehistoric 3''*, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, ISBN 4-8104-0926-0.

{{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Beppu, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

{{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Beppu, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=draft}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=draft}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=draft}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Supervised by the Historical Sites Research Committee of the Cultural Property Protection Division, Cultural Affairs Agency, *''Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan, Volume 3: Prehistoric 3''*, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, ISBN 4-8104-0926-0.
== External Links == * [http://bep-shounin-e.oita-ed.jp/gakkousyoukai/setumei/oninoiwaya.htm Onino Iwaya Kofun (Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School Official Site)] * {{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{panoViewer|OniNoIwaya Kofun-2, Funkyu 360-degree.jpg|2号墳の全天球画像}} '''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん、'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''とも）は、[[大分県]][[別府市]]上人西町にある[[古墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群]]」のうち）。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]後期（6世紀後半）の[[円墳]]2基からなる[[装飾古墳]]群。1号墳は[[別府市立上人小学校]]の敷地内にあり、2号墳もそのすぐそばにある。[[安山岩]]の巨石を組み上げた大きな石室を持つことが特徴である。
1号墳は、[[墳丘]]は流失しており、その大きさを推定すると、径約23メートル、高さ5メートルほどの円墳。石室は、全長9.2メートル、玄室は奥行き、幅とも2.3メートル、高さ2.9メートルの複室式の[[横穴式石室]]で、内部は全面が赤色に塗られ、前室には白色の[[鋸歯文]]が描かれている。また、近年、黄色や黒の三角文、蕨手文も確認されている。
2号墳は、1号墳の南西約80メートルにあり、直径約30メートル、高さ約6メートルの円墳である。石室は、単室の横穴式石室で、奥行き8メートル、玄室の天井高約4メートル。壁は石材をアーチ状に積層した構造を有する。玄室内部は、全面が赤色に塗装され、黒色で多数の[[円文]]、[[蕨文]]等が描かれている。
== 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群 *: 1957年（昭和32年）11月28日に「鬼ノ岩屋古墳」として国の史跡に指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2880|鬼ノ岩屋古墳}}</ref>。 *: 2017年（平成29年）2月9日に史跡範囲を追加指定し、指定名称を「鬼ノ岩屋・実相寺古墳群」に変更<ref>平成29年2月9日文部科学省告示第12号。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版 1991年 ISBN 4-8104-0926-0
== 外部リンク == * [http://bep-shounin-e.oita-ed.jp/gakkousyoukai/setumei/oninoiwaya.htm 鬼の岩屋古墳] * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2880|鬼ノ岩屋古墳}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{coord|33|18|56.4|N|131|29|32.3|E|region:JP-44|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おにのいわやこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:大分県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:別府市の歴史]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."

The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673383]] Wikidata name: Oni-no-iwa Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 6th century) and consists of two circular [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 6th century) and consists of two circular [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.

The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.

It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673383]] Wikidata name: Oni-no-iwa Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 6th century) and consists of two circular [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673383]] Wikidata name: Oni-no-iwa Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 6th century) and consists of two circular [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
