<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673383]] Wikidata name: Oni-no-iwa Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 6th century) and consists of two circular [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673383]] Wikidata name: Oni-no-iwa Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 6th century) and consists of two circular [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673383]] Wikidata name: Oni-no-iwa Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Beppu Daigaku Station|Beppu Daigaku Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Kamino-Nishimachi, [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 6th century) and consists of two circular [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Supervised by the Historical Sites Research Committee of the Cultural Property Protection Division, Cultural Affairs Agency, *''Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan, Volume 3: Prehistoric 3''*, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, ISBN 4-8104-0926-0.
== External Links == * [http://bep-shounin-e.oita-ed.jp/gakkousyoukai/setumei/oninoiwaya.htm Onino Iwaya Kofun (Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School Official Site)] * {{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}

The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Beppu Daigaku Station|en|Beppu Daigaku Station|WD=Q4890929|lt=Beppu Daigaku Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan}} facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Beppu Daigaku Station|en|Beppu Daigaku Station|WD=Q4890929|lt=Beppu Daigaku Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun]][[ja:鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]]
{{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan}} facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Beppu Daigaku Station|en|Beppu Daigaku Station|WD=Q4890929|lt=Beppu Daigaku Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Supervised by the Historical Sites Research Committee of the Cultural Property Protection Division, Cultural Affairs Agency, *''Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan, Volume 3: Prehistoric 3''*, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, ISBN 4-8104-0926-0.

The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Beppu Daigaku Station|en|Beppu Daigaku Station|WD=Q4890929|lt=Beppu Daigaku Station|1=Beppu Daigaku Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu|1=Beppu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>

<!--Oni-no-iwa Kofun--><!--Oni-no-iwa Kofun--><!--Oni-no-iwa Kofun--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11673383}} [[Category:Beppu, Ōita]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Oita prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Short description|Pair of ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Oni-no-iwa Kofun | native_name =鬼ノ岩屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oni-no-iwa Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.315667|N|131.492306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|56|N|131|29|32|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Oni-no-iwa Kofun'''|鬼ノ岩屋古墳|}} is a pair [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Shoninnakamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Beppu, Ōita|Beppu, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection extended in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139927|title=鬼ノ岩屋古墳・実相寺古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun are located halfway up a wide {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} facing Beppu Bay. Kofun No. 1 has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a vestibule and a house-shaped back chamber. Kofun No. 2 has a huge single burial chamber built by assembling {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} megaliths. Both are estimated to date from the late Kofun period. The external shape of Kofun No. 1 has been significantly deformed due to erosion, but it is estimated to have been 23 meters in diameter and approximately 5 meters in height. The entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, and the front chamber has white sawtooth patterns, as well as yellow and black triangular patterns and bracken patterns. Kofun No. 2 is located about 100 meters away from Kofun No. 1, and is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular mound with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about 6 meters. The burial chamber is 8 meters deep with a ceiling height of about 4 meters. The walls are made of stones piled up in an arch shape, and the entire interior of the burial chamber is painted red, with numerous circular and bracken patterns drawn in black. [[Decorated kofun]] of this style are extremely rare on the eastern Kyushu coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Beppu Daigaku Station|en|Beppu Daigaku Station|WD=Q4890929|lt=Beppu Daigaku Station|1=Beppu Daigaku Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> '''Onino Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, also known as 鬼の岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Kamino-Nishimachi, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu|1=Beppu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The site dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late 6th century) and consists of two circular {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} with decorative features. The tumuli are notable for their large stone chambers constructed with {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}} megaliths.
The first kofun is located within the grounds of {{ill|Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja|別府市立上人小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu City Shōnin Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the second is situated nearby. Both feature red-painted interiors and elaborate decorative patterns.
=== First Kofun === The mound of the first kofun has eroded, but it is estimated to have been a circular tumulus approximately 23 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. The {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} is a two-chambered {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) measuring 9.2 meters in length. The main chamber (玄室) has dimensions of 2.3 meters in depth and width, with a height of 2.9 meters. The entire interior is coated in red, with white {{ill|zigzag patterns|ja|鋸歯文|1=Sawtooth patterns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zigzag patterns|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawtooth patterns|qq=}} on the anterior wall. Recent studies have also revealed yellow and black triangular patterns and spirals.
=== Second Kofun === Located approximately 80 meters southwest of the first kofun, the second tumulus measures 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It features a single-chambered horizontal stone chamber, 8 meters in length, with a ceiling height of approximately 4 meters in the main chamber. The walls exhibit an arched construction technique, and the interior is entirely red-painted with numerous black circular and spiral patterns.
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * **Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster** ** Designated on November 28, 1957, as "Onino Iwaya Kofun." <ref>{{National Heritage Database|401|2880|Onino Iwaya Kofun}}</ref> ** On February 9, 2017, the designated area was expanded, and the name was updated to "Onino Iwaya and Jissōji Kofun Cluster." <ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 12, February 9, 2017.</ref>
